response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with strong software craftsmanship, but he does not meet the role's mandatory requirements. His hands-on C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus academic projects, far short of the required 5-10 years of Windows-focused C++ development. His database background is MySQL/MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and his career has centered on web, mobile, and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. He also lacks Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain expertise; RIDE Capital was a German fintech focused on investment structuring, not capital-markets systems.
strengths: Nearly two decades of broad software engineering experience with strong testing, TDD, and CI/CD practices
Systems-level exposure through low-latency DLang services and some professional C/C++ work at National Instruments
Excellent communication skills evidenced by workshops, conference talks, and course creation
concerns: C++ experience is brief (~7 months professionally) and roughly a decade old, versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL and MongoDB

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering fundamentals and CI/CD
